canonical_definition: The ability of an embedded watermark to remain detectable after the text has been altered by various corruptions, such as word substitution or being mixed with human-written text.
papers: On the Reliability of Watermarks for Large Language Models
Towards Codable Watermarking for Injecting Multi-Bits Information to LLMs
